title: Prince Louis Turns Eight: New Holiday Photo Released
summary: The Prince and Princess of Wales have released a new photograph to celebrate their youngest son Prince Louis's eighth birthday. The image shows Louis smiling and wearing a blue zip-up jumper, with his arms folded while standing on a boat during a family holiday in Cornwall. The photo was taken by Matt Porteous, a photographer who has previously worked with the royal family on several occasions, including Prince George's birthday portraits and the Wales family Christmas card in 2022. Prince Louis Arthur Charles was born on April 23, 2018, at St Mary's Hospital in London. He is the third child of Prince William and Catherine, after Prince George (12) and Princess Charlotte (10), and is fourth in line to the British throne. Known for his playful and cheeky behavior, Louis rarely accompanies his parents on official royal duties but is often seen at major events like Trooping the Colour and Easter Sunday services. The photo was shared on royal social media with the message: "Happy birthday, Louis! 8 today!" followed by a red balloon emoji. This release follows the family's tradition of sharing personal photos on their children's birthdays.

background_read: The British royal family often releases personal photos on birthdays to maintain public interest and a positive image. Prince Louis is fourth in line to the throne, after his grandfather King Charles III, his father Prince William, and his older brother Prince George.
Article_Structure: The main points of the article are the release of a new birthday photo of Prince Louis, details about the photo (location, photographer), and background information about Louis's birth and family. The purpose is to inform the public about the royal family's celebration and maintain engagement. The evidence comes from the royal family's social media post and known facts about the photographer's previous work. The author (BBC) is a credible news source. The methodology is straightforward reporting based on official releases.

perspectives.description: They use personal photos to connect with the public and show a happy family image.
The BBC reports on the photo as a news item, highlighting the family's tradition.
Many people enjoy seeing the royal children grow up through these photos.
